code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test04-FEB-2021Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating possible renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, bilirubinuria, and ketonuria, with low specific gravity and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal). HbA1c is low, suggesting no chronic hyperglycemia. Findings are suggestive of acute or chronic kidney dysfunction with possible tubular involvement; further nephrological evaluation is recommended.
